mnemonics.title: Amdahl's Law Formula
mnemonics.content: SPEED UP = 1 / (1 - P), where P = parallel fraction. Remember: 'Serial Part Is The Enemy' - no matter how many processors, limited by (1-P).

mnemonics.title: Three-Tier Architecture
mnemonics.content: Presentation → Logic → Data. Think 'PLD' or 'Presentation Loves Data indirectly through Business Logic.'

mnemonics.title: Gustafson's Law Key Point
mnemonics.content: 'Scale With Processors' - Gustafson assumes problem size increases with processor count, unlike Amdahl's fixed problem. GUS = Growing problem Size.

acronyms.usage: All processors run same code on different data elements simultaneously
Standard for distributed memory parallel programming
Architectural style for microservices API communication
Distributed systems can guarantee only 2 of 3 properties

visualTips.visualization: Draw a bar showing parallel portion (expandable with processors) vs serial portion (fixed). The serial portion acts as a ceiling on speedup.
Think of assembly line: Stage 1→Stage 2→Stage 3. After pipeline fills, output rate = 1 item/time unit despite multi-stage processing.
Data: Same operation, different data (matrix addition). Task: Different operations, same/different data (filter + sort + aggregate).

analogies.analogy: Like a car assembly line: one worker does frame, next does engine, next does paint—each car takes same total time but cars roll out continuously once pipeline fills.
Like a department of specialists: each has own inbox, processes one letter at a time, can hire assistants (spawn new actors), never directly touches another's desk (state).
Like newspaper subscription: publishers don't know subscribers, subscribers don't need publishers active—just drop in mailbox (topic), readers pick up when ready.
Like a restaurant kitchen: each station (grill, sauté, dessert) independent, communicates via tickets (API), can be fixed/added without shutting kitchen.
